title: Syracuse residents frustrated by delays in convent demolition
summary: Demolition of the former Maria Regina College convent complex remains stalled months after a March fire, frustrating northside residents and local officials. The project requires finalized agreements between current owner Mark Congel and Home Leasing (developer of a planned 181-unit housing project) before deconstruction can begin. Commissioner Michael Collins indicates paperwork is nearly complete, though Councilor Marty Nave has criticized Congel's property neglect and suggested the city may need to intervene.
department_refs: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Residents and city council member escalating frustration over delayed demolition and housing redevelopment; potential municipal intervention threatened if property owner inaction continues.
